app.title: Synaform
app.confirm_delete: Are you sure you want to delete?
app.name: Name

prompts.title: AI Prompts
prompts.subtitle:
These are the instructions Synaform sends to the AI. Adjust them to your workflow — your changes apply to all Collections. Available placeholders (replaced automatically): {today} = today's date, {month} = current month, {year} = current year, {language} = output language.
An empty text or the unchanged default resets a prompt to the shipped version.
prompts.reset_confirm: Discard your customized prompt and restore the shipped default?
prompts.extraction_rules_explain: Sent to the AI every time documents are read (“Read files & auto-fill”). Controls how values are extracted: output formats, the career-station table rules, how education entries are collected, and that the help text under each variable is treated as a binding instruction (e.g. a phone number format).
prompts.extraction_context_explain: Tells the AI what “today” is while it reads documents. Fields that refer to the recording itself (e.g. “Monat der Erfassung”, “generated_month”, “generated_year”) are filled from the current date instead of a date found in the documents, and missing language/location defaults are derived from the Collection.
prompts.generation_transform_explain: Only used when you type an optional command in the “Generate Docs” step (e.g. “Translate everything into Italian”, “Format the numbers the English way”). Defines how the AI transforms the final values right before they are written into the Word template. The stored dataset is never changed.

collections.title: Collections
collections.subtitle: A Collection bundles the questions you ask, the templates you fill, and every dataset you gather.
collections.empty_hint: Start by creating a Collection. Give it a purpose (e.g. “Candidate intake”), add the questions you need answered, upload a target template, and start collecting datasets.
collections.drafts_hint: Collections you started but haven’t set up yet. Resume any time.
collections.draft_discard_confirm: Discard the draft “{name}”? This cannot be undone.
collections.name_placeholder: e.g. Candidate profiles, Customer onboarding, Incident reports
collections.description_placeholder: One or two sentences about what this Collection is for. Shown at the top of the Collection page.
collections.language_hint: Influences how the AI reads documents and labels suggestions.
collections.help_what_is_body:
A Collection is a workspace for one use case. It contains:
• Variables — the questions/fields you want filled (“Name”, “Start date”, …)
• Target Templates — Word documents with {{placeholders}} that get filled automatically
• Datasets — every real case you process (candidate, customer, ticket, …)

Create one Collection per workflow. Keep them focused.

collection.tab_setup_subtitle: Define the variables you collect and upload the Word templates you want filled. You only touch this when the Collection changes.
collection.overview_step1: Decide which fields every dataset needs. Add designer options for lists, tables and checkboxes.
collection.overview_step2: Upload .docx templates that reference your variables as {{placeholders}}.
collection.overview_step3: Add sources: upload files, add URLs (LinkedIn, XING, web pages), or fill fields by hand.
collection.overview_step4: Pick a target template and generate the filled .docx. Export as CSV when you need the raw data elsewhere.
collection.overview_datasets_empty: No datasets in this Collection yet.
collection.summary_no_vars: No variables yet — define what data you want to collect.
collection.summary_no_templates: No Target Template yet — upload a .docx with {{placeholders}}.
collection.summary_ready: This Collection is ready to collect datasets.

variables.title: Variables & Placeholders
variables.subtitle: Variables are the questions you want answered for every dataset. Each variable can match a {{placeholder}} in your Target Templates.
variables.help_body: Variables are named slots (like “firstname” or “start_date”). When you generate a document, every {{variable}} in the template is replaced with the dataset’s value. The AI reads uploaded files and tries to fill these automatically, but you can always correct anything by hand.
variables.empty_hint: Add a few variables, or upload a Target Template first and we’ll detect placeholders automatically.
variables.from_template_hint: Scan a Target Template and turn its {{placeholders}} into variables.
variables.import_hint: Paste a variable list (e.g. from Word, Confluence, Excel) — AI turns it into variables.
variables.polish_hint: Use AI to suggest a clear description, example values, and “NOT this” hints for each variable. You review the diff before anything is saved.
variables.polish_modal_subtitle: Review the AI’s suggestions per variable. Tick the ones you want to apply, then click Apply.
variables.polish_no_sample: No sample dataset available — examples will be generic.
variables.polish_empty: The AI didn’t suggest changes for any variable. Try adding more variables or pasting a sample text in the next dataset.
variables.import_from_template_hint: Pick an uploaded Target Template and create variables directly from its {{placeholders}} — no AI guessing, exact mapping.
variables.import_no_templates: Upload a Target Template first (Templates tab).
variables.import_dialog_subtitle: We read the {{placeholders}} in your template and turn them into variables. Table groups and checkbox pairs are collapsed automatically.
variables.import_pick_template_hint: You can only import from templates already attached to this Collection.
variables.import_apply_none: Nothing selected — tick a row to add it
variables.import_summary: {total} placeholders → {tables} table(s), {checkboxes} checkbox(es), {lists} list(s), {texts} text field(s). {duplicate} already exist.
variables.field_hint_info: A short description helps the AI pick the right value from uploaded documents.
variables.designer_image_hint: Default rendering size (in pixels) for the embedded image. Templates can override via {{key:width=…:height=…}}.
variables.designer_image_placement_inline: Inline — image flows with the text at the placeholder
variables.designer_image_placement_float: Floating — anchored to the page, text wraps around it
variables.designer_image_float_hint: Position and wrapping apply only when “Floating” is selected. Example: photo in the upper right corner = Right + Top.
variables.column_type_hint: Pick a type per column. 'List' renders as proper bullet paragraphs inside the cell (one bullet per line you enter).
variables.col_list_format_flat: Every entry as a bullet point
variables.col_list_format_hint:
“Every entry as a bullet point” renders each line with a bullet — consistent, predictable lists.
“Structured” detects date ranges (bold, no bullet) and treats the first line of each block as a title without a bullet; only the task lines get bullets. Use it for the classic HR profile layout.
variables.col_bullet_hint: Choose the bullet shape (e.g. ■ square, ♥) and its indent. A custom shape is applied even when the Word template defines no bullet list — great for square N&H bullets. Leave on Default to keep the template’s bullet.
variables.confirm_delete_field: Remove this variable from the Collection?
variables.designer_list_hint: Tune how this list looks in the generated document.
variables.designer_prevent_orphans: Keep items together across page breaks
variables.designer_prevent_orphans_hint: Adds a keepNext rule so the list doesn’t end with a single dangling item at the top of the next page.
variables.designer_list_spacing_hint: Insert an empty line just before the first item or just after the last one.
variables.designer_list_top_blank: Add an empty line above the list
variables.designer_list_bottom_blank: Add an empty line below the list
variables.designer_table_hint: Tune how this table behaves when it spans multiple pages.
variables.designer_repeat_header: Repeat header row on every page
variables.designer_prevent_row_break: Prevent rows from splitting across pages
variables.designer_keep_with_prev: Keep table glued to the paragraph above
variables.designer_checkbox_hint: Controls how this checkbox appears in the generated document. Tip: with the pair {{checkb.key.yes}} / {{checkb.key.no}} the template always renders checkbox glyphs — if you want plain text (“Ja” / “Nein”) like a normal text field, put the plain {{key}} placeholder in the Word template instead; the labels below are used for it.
variables.designer_checkbox_clickable: Make it a clickable Word checkbox
variables.designer_checkbox_clickable_hint: Inserts a real Word content control so the user can toggle the checkbox in Word 2010+. Disable to fall back to a static ☒ / ☐ glyph.
variables.designer_checkbox_glyphs_hint: Used when the template carries a {{checkb.X.yes}} / {{checkb.X.no}} pair. Defaults to ☒ / ☐.
variables.designer_checkbox_labels_hint: Used when the template carries a plain {{X}} placeholder (not the glyph pair). Defaults to Ja / Nein.

templates.title: Target Templates
templates.subtitle: Upload the Word documents you want filled. Use {{variable_name}} anywhere in the document and we’ll replace it.
templates.help_body:
Open your .docx in Word and type {{firstname}} wherever the first name should appear. Save, upload it here, and every dataset will generate a filled copy in one click.

Tip: supported placeholders include plain text, lists, checkboxes, images, and repeating rows.
templates.empty_hint: Upload a Word document (.docx) with {{placeholders}}. We’ll scan it and match placeholders to your variables.
templates.drop_hint: Drop a .docx here, or click to browse
templates.match_all_covered: All placeholders are covered by your variables.
templates.match_checkbox_plain: renders as plain "Ja" / "Nein" text (not a glyph)
templates.tip_checkbox_plain: Variable {key} is a checkbox, but the template uses the plain form. For a proper checkmark use a pair like {pair} in the Word document.
templates.tip_list_unused: List variable {key} is declared but never used in this template.
templates.tip_image_unused: Image variable {key} is declared but never used in this template.
templates.language_hint: AI extraction will write values in this language. Falls back to the Collection language if unset.
templates.lint_uploaded_with_findings: Template uploaded — doctor flagged some items
templates.lint_dialog_subtitle: Found {errors} error(s), {warnings} warning(s), {infos} info note(s) across {placeholders} placeholders.
templates.lint_dialog_footer: Errors mean the engine WILL produce broken output — please fix them in Word. Warnings mean the layout may be sub-optimal. Infos are auto-fixed by the engine but worth tidying up in the source.

datasets.title: Datasets
datasets.subtitle: Every dataset is one case: one candidate, one customer, one ticket. The gold of your Collection.
datasets.empty_hint: Create a dataset, fill in what you know, upload source documents, and let the AI do the rest.
datasets.name_placeholder: e.g. candidate name or project title
datasets.new_hint: After creating you can fill the variables by hand, upload source documents, and generate filled templates.
datasets.confirm_delete: Delete this dataset and all of its data?
datasets.retention_hint: GDPR: automatically remove this dataset N months after the last update.
datasets.retention_overdue_hint: These datasets have reached the auto-delete date you configured. Delete them now to honour your data-retention policy.
datasets.retention_delete_confirm: This will permanently delete {count} dataset(s) along with their files and AI extractions. Continue?
datasets.section_data_hint: Edit any field below. Photos, lists, and tables get type-aware editors.
datasets.section_files_hint: Upload PDFs, images, Office files — they’ll be read and matched to your variables.
datasets.section_extraction_hint: Runs the extraction model against all source files.
datasets.section_generate_hint: Pick a Target Template and click Generate. The filled .docx is yours.
datasets.image_confirm_remove: Remove the photo for this variable?
datasets.analyze_status_running_ai: AI is reading the extracted text...
datasets.analyze_status_image_hint: Images are processed with Vision AI — about 5–15 seconds per image.
datasets.analyze_status_doc_hint: Office documents are extracted with Apache Tika first; if that fails the AI takes over (slower).
datasets.analyze_dont_close: Please keep this tab open until the analysis finishes.
datasets.extract_hint: Extracts answers from your source files using AI. Re-run anytime.
datasets.parse_failed: AI couldn’t auto-fill the form. Try again or switch the chat model in Settings.
datasets.parse_unusable_shape: AI returned a response we couldn’t apply to the form. Existing field values were left unchanged.
datasets.parse_partial: {ok} of {total} field groups extracted. Failed: {failed}. Other fields were filled successfully.
datasets.parse_summary_skipped: {ok} of {total} field groups extracted, {skipped} skipped (already filled).
datasets.parse_hint: AI reads all uploaded files and pre-fills matching fields.
datasets.parse_timeout: Reading took too long and was stopped. The selected AI model is likely too slow — switch the extraction model to a faster one in Settings and try again.
datasets.parse_deadline: Reading stopped at the time limit before every field was processed. The selected AI model is slow — switch to a faster extraction model in Settings, then run it again to fill the rest.
datasets.parse_unreadable_warning: Couldn’t read: {files}. Check the file or re-upload it — the other documents were still processed.
datasets.parse_blocked_saving: Please wait for the save to finish before reading files.
datasets.file_unreadable_hint: This file could not be read — try a different format (e.g. PDF) or re-upload it.
datasets.save_blocked_parsing: Please wait for “Read files & auto-fill” to finish before saving.
datasets.generate_instruction_placeholder: Optional command — e.g. “Translate everything into Italian”, “Format the numbers the English way”
datasets.generate_instruction_hint: The AI applies this command to all values right before the document is filled — e.g. translate, reformat numbers/dates, change the tone. The saved dataset stays untouched. Leave empty for a normal generation.
datasets.generate_no_vars: Fill the form or run AI extraction to populate variables before generating.
datasets.confirm_delete_source_file: Remove this source file? Extracted data is kept.
datasets.col_relevant_hint: Tick to mark this position as relevant — selected rows are collected into the {{relevant_positions}} list for page 1.
datasets.url_add_hint: Synaform fetches the page, strips markup, and feeds the text to AI alongside uploaded files. Works best for public profile pages (LinkedIn, XING, GitHub, company sites).

preview.title: Live preview
preview.no_template: Upload a Target Template to see a live preview.
preview.render_pdf_hint: Generate the actual .docx, convert it to PDF, and show the authoritative rendering.
preview.pdf_unavailable: True-preview PDF requires libreoffice on the backend host. The HTML live preview above still works; add libreoffice (or a gotenberg sidecar) to enable the PDF path.

dashboard.title: System info
dashboard.tika_role: Local document text extractor (PDF, DOCX, XLSX, PPTX…).
dashboard.ai_chat_role: Reads source documents and fills variables. One call per dataset.
dashboard.ai_vision_role: Reads text from JPG/PNG scans and low-quality PDFs. One call per image.
dashboard.cost_hint: Tip: image scans are the dominant cost — each JPG/PNG triggers one Vision-AI roundtrip (typically 5–60 s). Document files (PDF/DOCX/…) go through local Tika first and only fall back to Vision-AI when Tika returns empty/low-quality text.

export.title: Export Datasets
export.subtitle: Download all datasets in this Collection as a flat CSV — one row per dataset, one column per variable.
export.help_body: The CSV contains all resolved variables (your answers + AI values + overrides) plus metadata columns: dataset name, status, created, updated, source document count, generated document count.
export.encoding_hint: UTF-8 encoded, comma-separated, RFC 4180 quoted. Opens in Excel, Numbers, Google Sheets.

danger.title: Danger Zone
danger.subtitle: Deleting a Collection is permanent. All variables, datasets, generated documents and source files are removed.
danger.warning_with_datasets: This Collection contains {count} dataset(s). Deletion will remove them all.
danger.confirm_body: Type the Collection name exactly to confirm:

wizard.title: Set up Collection
wizard.chooser_title: How do you want to start?
wizard.chooser_subtitle: Pick the closest match — you can always switch paths from the manual editor later.
wizard.mode_template_hint: Upload your .docx and we’ll derive variables from its {{placeholders}}.
wizard.mode_from_doc_hint: Upload any .docx and the AI will propose variables AND insert {{placeholders}} into a templated copy for you.
wizard.mode_no_template_title: I don’t have a template yet
wizard.mode_no_template_hint: Start from a pasted list of fields (AI parses it) or add variables by hand.
wizard.basics_subtitle: Give it a clear purpose. You can change all of this later.
wizard.template_subtitle: Drop the .docx that should be filled. We’ll scan its {{placeholders}} so the next step is pre-populated.
wizard.template_skip: I’ll do this later — skip for now
wizard.fields_template_subtitle: We detected these placeholders in your template. Untick anything you don’t need.
wizard.fields_template_missing: No template uploaded — skip back to the previous step.
wizard.fields_text_title: Add the variables you want to collect
wizard.fields_text_subtitle: Paste a list (one field per line, or comma-separated) and we’ll turn it into typed variables.
wizard.fields_text_empty: Nothing parsed yet. You can also finish empty and add variables in the manual editor.
wizard.from_doc_subtitle: Drop any .docx — the AI will identify what should be variables and insert {{placeholders}} into a copy of the document for you.
wizard.from_doc_drop_subhint: The AI will read it and propose variables. A new templated copy is saved automatically.
wizard.from_doc_uploading: Reading your document and proposing variables…
wizard.ai_suggest_status_extracting: Reading the document text and detecting the document type…
wizard.ai_suggest_status_proposing: AI is identifying which words should become variables—this is the longest step (≈ 5–20 s).
wizard.ai_suggest_status_finalizing: Inserting placeholders into a templated copy and saving…
wizard.ai_suggest_hint: Larger documents are scanned in overlapping windows, so multi-page drafts take longer.
wizard.ai_suggest_dont_close: Please keep this tab open until the suggestions appear.
wizard.from_doc_manual_hint: Open the templated .docx and replace the highlighted text with the placeholder by hand.
wizard.from_doc_empty: The AI didn’t propose any variables — try a different document or use the manual path.
wizard.from_doc_fields_subtitle: Untick anything you don’t want. Items marked “Inserted in template” are already wired into your new .docx.
wizard.review_subtitle: Your Collection is set up. Add the first dataset now, or open the Collection to fine-tune everything first.
wizard.review_hint: Tip: the manual editor under “Set up” lets you tweak field types and template attachments any time.

setup.menu_wizard_hint: Walk through the whole setup step by step.
setup.menu_from_template_hint: Upload a .docx with {{placeholders}} — we’ll derive the variables.
setup.menu_from_doc: Convert a draft document with AI
setup.menu_from_doc_hint: Upload a .docx without placeholders — the AI proposes variables and writes the {{placeholders}} for you.
setup.menu_from_text: Start from a pasted variable list
setup.menu_from_text_hint: Paste a list of fields and the AI will turn them into variables.
setup.menu_manual_hint: Skip the wizard and edit Variables and Templates by hand.

settings.title: Settings
settings.company_name_hint: Shown as branding on generated documents when templates use {{company_name}}.
settings.extraction_model_hint: AI model used to read source documents. Leave on default to follow your account setting.
settings.validation_model_hint: Optional second-pass AI to double-check extracted data. Leave empty to skip.
